---
title: "MLE shape diagnostics — skewness and excess kurtosis of the estimator, over (interval, noise) by N_ch"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
WHY THIS EXISTS, and what it does NOT claim.

The distortion machinery of Figures 2 and 5 is a SECOND-MOMENT framework: it compares the covariance
the likelihood reports with the covariance the estimates actually have. That comparison presumes a
covariance is an adequate summary of the estimator's uncertainty, i.e. that the sampling distribution
of the MLE is close to normal.

At few channels and short intervals it is not. This figure measures how far, over the same design
plane the validity maps use, so the region where an ellipse (and therefore a reported confidence
interval) means what it says can be drawn rather than assumed.

THE DISTINCTION THAT MUST NOT BE LOST: a non-normal MLE distribution is NOT a defect of the
likelihood. A perfectly correct likelihood produces a skewed MLE at small samples; normality is an
asymptotic property of the ESTIMATOR, not a claim of the model. So this is a limitation that sits
ABOVE the paper's subject: even where the likelihood reports its own information honestly (J = G),
a 95% interval built from any covariance can fail to cover 95%, because its validity rests on
asymptotic normality rather than on the second moment being right.

Two group sizes are shown because the group size is what controls the approach to normality: each
estimate is fitted to `group_size` recordings, so the departure shrinks roughly as 1/sqrt(group).
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse); library(patchwork)
```

```{r params}
ALGO   <- "macro_IR"
NCHS   <- c(10, 50, 200, 1000)
NOISES <- c("0.05", "0.1", "0.2", "0.5", "1", "10")
GROUPS <- c(10, 100)
DATA   <- "../data/1c2ae6f"
CACHE  <- "figure_normality_shape_cache.csv"

PARAMS <- c(off = "log[10]~k[off]", Num_ch_mean = "log[10]~N[ch]",
            on = "log[10]~k[on]", unitary_current = "log[10]~i")
```

```{r compute}
# ~1.7 GB of cloud CSVs, so the per-cell moments are cached; delete the cache to recompute.
if (file.exists(CACHE)) {
  shp <- read.csv(CACHE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
} else {
  skew <- function(x) { x <- x[is.finite(x)]; mean((x - mean(x))^3) / sd(x)^3 }
  exk  <- function(x) { x <- x[is.finite(x)]; mean((x - mean(x))^4) / var(x)^2 - 3 }
  shp <- expand_grid(nch = NCHS, z = NOISES) %>%
    pmap_dfr(function(nch, z) {
      f <- sprintf("%s/figure_3_G_nch_%d_nsim_10000_%s_noise_%s_mle_cloud_runs.csv", DATA, nch, ALGO, z)
      if (!file.exists(f)) return(NULL)
      read.csv(f, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
        filter(component_path == "Model_Parameters_Hat", statistic == "value",
               group_size %in% GROUPS, param_name %in% names(PARAMS)) %>%
        group_by(group_size, interval_in_tau, param_name) %>%
        summarise(n = n(), skew = skew(value), exkurt = exk(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
        mutate(Num_ch = nch, noise = z)
    })
  write.csv(shp, CACHE, row.names = FALSE)
}
shp <- shp %>%
  mutate(lx = log10(interval_in_tau), ly = log10(as.numeric(noise)),
         nchf = factor(paste0("N[ch]==", Num_ch), levels = paste0("N[ch]==", NCHS)),
         prow = factor(sprintf("%s~~~(g==%d)", PARAMS[param_name], group_size),
                       levels = as.vector(outer(PARAMS[c("off","Num_ch_mean")], GROUPS,
                                                function(p, g) sprintf("%s~~~(g==%d)", p, g)))))
cat("cells:", nrow(shp), " | group sizes:", paste(sort(unique(shp$group_size)), collapse = ", "),
    " | fits per cell:", paste(sort(unique(shp$n)), collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 8}
RAMP <- c("#021024","#053061","#2166AC","#4393C3","#92C5DE","#D1E5F0","#F7F7F7",
          "#FDDBC7","#F4A582","#D6604D","#B2182B","#67001F","#3d0011")
bandcols <- function(breaks) {
  n <- length(breaks) - 1; ctr <- findInterval(0, breaks); nb <- ctr - 1; nr <- n - ctr; K <- max(nb, nr, 1)
  blue <- colorRampPalette(rev(RAMP[1:7]), space = "Lab")(K + 1)
  red  <- colorRampPalette(RAMP[7:13],     space = "Lab")(K + 1)
  cols <- rep(RAMP[7], n)
  if (nb > 0) cols[seq(ctr - 1, 1)] <- blue[2:(nb + 1)]
  if (nr > 0) cols[seq(ctr + 1, n)] <- red[2:(nr + 1)]
  cols
}
SK_BRK <- c(-2,-1,-0.5,-0.25,-0.1,0.1,0.25,0.5,1,2,4); SK_PAL <- bandcols(SK_BRK)
KU_BRK <- c(-2,-1,-0.5,-0.25,-0.1,0.1,0.25,0.5,1,2,5); KU_PAL <- bandcols(KU_BRK)
X_BRK <- log10(c(.01,.1,1)); Y_BRK <- log10(c(.05,.5,10))

blk <- function(v, brk, pal, top = FALSE, bottom = FALSE) {
  d <- shp %>% filter(param_name %in% c("off","Num_ch_mean")) %>%
    mutate(zz = pmin(pmax(.data[[v]], min(brk)), max(brk)))
  ggplot(d, aes(lx, ly, z = zz)) +
    geom_contour_filled(breaks = brk) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal, drop = FALSE, guide = "none") +
    geom_contour(breaks = c(-0.5, 0.5), colour = "black", linewidth = .3) +
    geom_point(size = .08, colour = "grey35", alpha = .35) +
    facet_grid(prow ~ nchf, labeller = label_parsed) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = X_BRK, labels = c(".01",".1","1")) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = Y_BRK, labels = c(".05",".5","10")) +
    labs(y = "noise") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 8, base_family = "Helvetica") +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(.12,"cm"),
          legend.position = "none", axis.text = element_text(size = 6),
          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8), strip.text.y = element_text(size = 6.5, angle = 0),
          plot.margin = margin(2,2,2,2)) +
    (if (top) theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 7.5)) else theme(strip.text.x = element_blank())) +
    (if (bottom) labs(x = expression(Delta %.% k[off])) else
       theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank()))
}
cbar <- function(pal, lab, title) {
  n <- length(pal)
  ggplot(data.frame(x = seq_len(n)), aes(x, 1, fill = factor(x))) +
    geom_tile(colour = "grey92", linewidth = .15) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal, guide = "none") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0.5, n + 0.5, 1), labels = lab, expand = c(0,0)) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.5, 2.0), clip = "off") + labs(y = title) +
    theme_void(base_family = "Helvetica") +
    theme(axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, size = 7, hjust = 1, vjust = .5, margin = margin(r = 4)),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 5.5), plot.margin = margin(3, 26, 2, 40))
}
btitle <- function(t) ggplot() + theme_void(base_family="Helvetica") +
  annotate("text", x=0, y=0, label=t, hjust=0, size=2.9, fontface="bold") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,1), clip="off") + theme(plot.margin=margin(3,2,0,26))

g <- btitle("A   skewness of the MLE marginal") / blk("skew", SK_BRK, SK_PAL, top = TRUE) /
     cbar(SK_PAL, formatC(SK_BRK, format="g", digits=2), "skewness") /
     btitle("B   excess kurtosis of the MLE marginal") / blk("exkurt", KU_BRK, KU_PAL, bottom = TRUE) /
     cbar(KU_PAL, formatC(KU_BRK, format="g", digits=2), "excess kurtosis") +
     plot_layout(heights = c(0.16, 4, 0.28, 0.16, 4.2, 0.28))
g
ggsave("Figure_normality_shape.pdf", g, width = 7.0, height = 8)
```

```{r numbers}
cat("\n=== where the Gaussian summary is defensible (|skew| < 0.5 and |exkurt| < 0.5) ===\n")
print(as.data.frame(shp %>% filter(param_name %in% c("off","Num_ch_mean")) %>%
  group_by(param_name, group_size, Num_ch) %>%
  summarise(frac_ok = mean(abs(skew) < 0.5 & abs(exkurt) < 0.5), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = frac_ok)), digits = 2, row.names = FALSE)
```
